human_readable_summary: This ledger records what backprop-trace's V8/Node 22 engine (src/general-engine.ts) produces for one SGD step on the XOR-sigmoid 2-2-1 topology with operator-chosen deterministic initial weights, and what claim values that engine reproduction pins. Unlike the Mazur fixture (which has a widely-cited published anchor for post-update total error), no canonical XOR step-1 trace exists in the literature for this exact initial-weight configuration — the trace is engine-anchored. Goodfellow et al. 2016 Ch. 6.1 publishes a CONVERGED XOR solution (W=[[1,1],[1,1]], c=[0,-1], w=[1,-2], b=0, ReLU activation, loss=0) but does not provide an arbitrary-init step-1 trace; this fixture establishes one for the sigmoid + half-squared-error setting.
engine_reproduction_source: fixtures/xor.golden.jsonl is the engine-emitted first-run output of src/general-engine.ts on XOR_INPUT (V8/Node 22).

source.anchor: engine-anchored (no published step-1 trace exists for XOR-sigmoid 2-2-1 with these specific initial weights)

converged_reference.author: Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville
converged_reference.title: Deep Learning, Ch. 6.1 — Example: Learning XOR
converged_reference.summary: Provides the canonical analytical CONVERGED solution for XOR (ReLU, loss=0). Does NOT provide an arbitrary-init step-1 trace. This fixture's engine-anchored step-1 values are not directly comparable to Goodfellow's converged values; the converged reference is cited for educational context only.

operator_chosen_init.rationale: Deterministic small monotonically-increasing weights (0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4 for input-to-hidden; 0.5, 0.6 for hidden-to-output; 0.1 for both biases). Makes the trace reproducible byte-for-byte across runs of the engine on V8/Node 22 and human-readable for pedagogy. No standard 'XOR step-1 trace' exists; this fixture establishes one.

hyperparameters.bias_policy: constant (per v0.3 design; bias updates deferred to v0.4)

claim_type_legend.source_published_input: Constant input or hyperparameter explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_loss: Loss value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_forward: Forward-pass value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_update: Post-update parameter value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.widely_cited_downstream_anchor: Value commonly repeated in downstream pedagogy; not given an explicit derivation in the source at this precision
claim_type_legend.engine_anchored: No published source provides this value; the engine's first-run output is the canonical anchor

reproduction_status_legend.verified: Engine reproduces this value within tolerance
reproduction_status_legend.drift_observed: Engine produces a different value; documented in drift_note. Hard-gate value (true/false) governs whether drift fails the build or merely WARNs.

claims.provenance: Half squared error of (target_y - forward.y.out) computed on the pre-update forward pass. Engine value pinned at 9 significant digits per numeric_policy.precision_significant_digits.

Post-update total error after one SGD step from the operator-chosen init. Engine value pinned at 9 significant digits. Decrease vs pre-update loss_total demonstrates the step moved in the descent direction (0.0525817333 -> 0.0508414178, delta -0.0017403155).
claims.v0_3_behavior: Engine-anchored: any drift here would mean the engine math or canonical emission changed. bp verify general fails (hard gate) on drift.

claims.provenance: sigmoid(net_h1) where net_h1 = w_x1_h1*x1 + w_x2_h1*x2 + b_hidden = 0.1*1 + 0.2*0 + 0.1 = 0.2.
sigmoid(net_h2) where net_h2 = w_x1_h2*x1 + w_x2_h2*x2 + b_hidden = 0.3*1 + 0.4*0 + 0.1 = 0.4.
sigmoid(net_y) where net_y = w_h1_y*out_h1 + w_h2_y*out_h2 + b_output. Single-output topology (XOR is a binary classification reducing to one scalar output).
Output error signal for y: (target_y - out_y) * sigmoid'(net_y) = 0.324289171 * 0.219125705. Descent-direction sign convention (gradient absorbs the sign, update = +lr*gradient).
Post-update forward pass through the same network with parameters_after. Confirms the SGD step pushed the y output closer to target=1 (0.675710829 -> 0.681122538, delta +0.005411709).

standards_referenced: Pineau et al. JMLR 2021 — ML Reproducibility Checklist (https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.12206)
Mitchell et al. FAT* 2019 — Model Cards (https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993)
Gebru et al. CACM 2021 — Datasheets for Datasets (https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09010)
Kapoor et al. Science Advances 2024 — REFORMS (https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adk3452)
Jia et al. IEEE S&P 2021 — Proof-of-Learning (https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2103.05633)
Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville 2016 — Deep Learning Ch. 6.1 (https://www.deeplearningbook.org/contents/mlp.html)

caveats_and_recommendations.scope: single-step XOR with operator-chosen deterministic init (v0.3 SCOPE)
caveats_and_recommendations.activation_hidden: sigmoid (Math.exp via V8 fdlibm port; deterministic on Node 22 V8)
caveats_and_recommendations.optimizer: sgd (no momentum, no Adam, no weight decay)
caveats_and_recommendations.bias_policy: constant in step 1 (per v0.3 design; bias updates deferred to v0.4)
caveats_and_recommendations.cross_engine_portability: V8 only; Hermes / JSC / Bun-JSC untested
caveats_and_recommendations.cross_node_major_portability: tested on Node 22.x only; Math.exp is not contractually bit-stable across V8 majors
caveats_and_recommendations.epoch_scope: ONE training sample, ONE SGD step. Convergence to the 4-row XOR truth table requires many steps across all 4 training samples — that is outside v0.3 scope and is what Goodfellow's converged-solution reference addresses analytically. v0.3 ships only the canonical first-step trace from the operator-chosen init.
